summary: Recorded the successful 2026-03-26 production apply of the repo-managed n8n runtime on docker-runtime-lv3 with PostgreSQL persistence on postgres-lv3 and protected editor publication through the shared NGINX edge, then carried that live evidence forward into repo release 0.174.0 after a latest-main runtime and edge revalidation completed successfully.

verification.observed: `make live-apply-service service=n8n env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` completed successfully from the current main-aligned integration worktree with `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=123 changed=6 failed=0`, `nginx-lv3 : ok=42 changed=4 failed=0`, `postgres-lv3 : ok=41 changed=0 failed=0`, and `localhost : ok=16 changed=0 failed=0`.

After rebasing onto the latest `origin/main`, `ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/n8n.yml --private-key /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e env=production -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump --limit 'postgres-lv3,docker-runtime-lv3,nginx-lv3'` completed successfully with `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=119 changed=2 failed=0`, `nginx-lv3 : ok=41 changed=4 failed=0`, and `postgres-lv3 : ok=41 changed=0 failed=0`, while intentionally skipping the already-live localhost DNS play because this shell did not export `HETZNER_DNS_API_TOKEN`.

verification.check: Rendered runtime database host and local health
verification.observed: `sudo grep '^DB_POSTGRESDB_HOST=' /run/lv3-secrets/n8n/runtime.env` on `docker-runtime-lv3` returned `DB_POSTGRESDB_HOST=10.10.10.50`, and local `http://127.0.0.1:5678/healthz` plus `http://127.0.0.1:5678/healthz/readiness` both returned `{"status":"ok"}`.

verification.check: Public edge health endpoint and DNS resolution
verification.observed: `dig +short n8n.example.com` returned `203.0.113.1`, and `curl -fsSI https://n8n.example.com/healthz` returned `HTTP/2 200` through the shared NGINX edge.

notes: The first mainline attempt on 2026-03-25 was blocked by a Hetzner DNS brownout window and concurrent Proxmox host reachability failures.

The successful 2026-03-26 rerun narrowed DNS reconciliation to the n8n-owned record, regenerated the shared static portal artifacts required by edge publication, and preserved the shared edge auth contract for langfuse while publishing the final n8n route.

Repo release 0.174.0 preserves the same live receipt after `origin/main` advanced with unrelated integration work; the latest-main revalidation reconverged postgres, runtime, and edge from the rebased tree without needing to mutate the already-live `n8n.example.com` DNS record.
